ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Continued To Apr-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 287375 The William H. Quayle Trust.

Robert Peterson (Trustee) in Propria Persona. Marky Quayle (Daughter) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Peterson explained that the rents for October of 2008 were collected by Marky Quayle because the previous Trustee had allowed her to collect the rents, that this was a transition period as he was appointed in mid-September and that the rents were now payable to the Trusts. In addition, he stated that Marky has contacts, that she over sees the repairs needed for the property, has the work done, pays with her credit card, that he has the invoices for all of the reimbursements and that this saves the Trust money. Furthermore, he stated that the antique furniture was in Phyllis Quayle's possession in her home, that not all of the items were turned over to him, that some of the items were missing, that they could possibly be in the storage room or stolen, that the jewelry was never turned over to him and that the receipt from Andrea Leung showed that the jewlery was in Phillis' possession. Mr. Peterson is to submit the invoices for Marky Quayle's reimbursements. Both matters were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2010 Continued To Jan-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2009 Continued To Jan-05-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 287375 (The William H. Quayle Trust).

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Andrea Leung. Robert Peterson, Trustee in Propria Persona. Gregory O'Keeffe for the Conservatee. Marky Quayle in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Marky Quayle that she needed to appear through Counsel. The Parties adressed and presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to all of the issues raised in the Examiner's notes regarding both matters. The Court took both matters under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Final Account Of The Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-30-2008 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-19-2008 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trusee, For Appointment Of Successor Trustee And For Approval Of Dual Compensation
Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called with case #287375 (The William H. Quayle Trust).

Appearances: Robert Peterson in Pro Per. Marta McNeill for and with Marky Quayle. Gregory O'Keeffe for Phylis Quayle (Conservatee/Beneficiary).

The Court informed Mr. Peterson that it would not allow the Trustees fees to be charged at the attorney rate of $250.00 per hour. After hearing oral argument, the Court took the issues regarding fees under submission. Ms. McNeill expressed concern about Peter Quayle residing in the property located on Broadway Street stating that his art work was being stored through out the premises, that the premises could be rented out to benefit the Conservatee, and proposed renting an apartment nearby so that Peter could be close to his Mother and continue to visit with her. The Court continued the issues regarding Peter Quayle to November 18, 2008. The Court ordered all art work be removed from the premises as well as the residence. If the property is not maintained and kept clear, the Court will consider Ms. McNeill's suggestion.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Linda Forster CSR#13286. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.
